programs.title: Seinfeld
programs.desc: Jerry, George and Elaine have a harrowing experience trying to get a table without reservations at a crowded Chinese restaurant.

programs.title: Seinfeld
programs.desc: After mustering enough courage to call a girl for a second date, George repeatedly gets her answering machine then blurts out a nasty message. Regretting it later, he talks Jerry into helping get rid of the message before it can be heard.

programs.title: Seinfeld
programs.desc: Jerry has second thoughts after helping Elaine get an apartment in his building.

programs.title: Seinfeld
programs.desc: Elaine and Jerry are left stranded at a party after George goes home with his date.

programs.title: Seinfeld
programs.desc: Elaine talks Jerry into hiring a friend who's working his way through college by cleaning people's homes. When a statue is discovered missing, he suspects the student. But Jerry is unsure how to handle getting it back.

programs.title: Seinfeld
programs.desc: George, ever the hypochondriac, thinks he's having a heart attack and, at Kramer's urging, seeks the advice of a "holistic" healer.
